title: Product Costing Analyst (Manufacturing) (Jurong East)
description:
Office Location: Jurong East (on-site)

A client in manufacturing and engineering business is looking to hire a Product Costing Analyst to join its finance function in Singapore. This role sits at the intersection of finance, operations, and commercial teams, supporting cost transparency, pricing decisions, and margin management in a competitive, product-driven environment.

Job Description

  • Analyse full product cost structures, including materials, labor, and manufacturing overheads, to ensure accurate and reliable costing.
  • Review standard versus actual costs, investigate variances, and identify key cost drivers affecting margins.
  • Maintain and improve product costing models, methodologies, and standards to ensure consistency across sites.
  • Work with ERP and costing systems to enhance cost allocation logic and overall data quality.
  • Partner with production, procurement, and sales teams to gather inputs and validate cost assumptions.
  • Provide cost analysis to support pricing decisions, margin evaluation, and commercial discussions.
  • Support budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning by supplying product cost inputs and assumptions.
  • Prepare regular management reports on product costs, trends, risks, and performance.
  • Identify opportunities for cost optimisation and process improvement within the costing framework.
  • Ensure costing practices comply with accounting standards and support audit requirements.
  • Perform scenario and sensitivity analysis to assess the impact of changes in materials, labour, or overhead.
  • Deliver ad-hoc costing analysis for tenders, special projects, and strategic initiatives such as make-vs-buy or footprint optimization.

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, Economics, or a related discipline. Professional certifications (e.g. CPA, CMA) or a postgraduate degree are advantageous.
  • Around 3–5 years of experience in cost accounting, product costing, or financial analysis within a manufacturing environment.
  • Hands-on experience with ERP or costing systems (e.g. SAP, Oracle, or equivalent) and strong Excel capabilities.
  • Fluent in Mandarin Chinese, both written and verbal.
  • Detail-oriented, data-driven, and comfortable working with complex cost structures.
  • Able to explain financial insights clearly to non-finance stakeholders.
  • Works well across functions and builds effective working relationships.
  • Capable of managing multiple workstreams while meeting tight timelines.
